title: Vignea × mithala
taxonomy: Vignea × mithala is a hybrid grapevine, the result of crossing Vitis vinifera and Vitis amurensis. It is a type of wine grape used in viticulture and winemaking. This grapevine is valued for its resistance to disease and ability to thrive in cooler climates. The classification of Vignea × mithala falls under the species Vitis vinifera and its hybrids. It is often used in the production of white and sparkling wines.

properties: Vignea × mithala has been reported to possess various therapeutic properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. These properties are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids in the plant. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit potential in treating various health conditions, including infections, wounds, and inflammatory diseases. Additionally, Vignea × mithala has been studied for its antidiabetic and anticancer properties, showing promise in the development of new therapeutic agents. Further research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic potential of Vignea × mithala.
